question: Is the sapphire ring placed specifically on the ring finger (the fourth digit) of the X-ray hand?
Is the ring positioned on the proximal phalanx (the bone segment closest to the palm), consistent with standard ring-wearing anatomy?
Is the metal band of the ring rendered with an X-ray effect (bright white/radiopaque), matching the visual density of the hand's bones?
Does the gemstone portion retain its original photorealistic appearance, specifically displaying the true blue color and sparkling facets?
Is the blue color strictly confined to the gemstone, ensuring the metal band and hand remain in the monochromatic X-ray palette?
Is the gemstone shape clearly recognizable as the large oval cut from the reference ring image?
Are the skeletal structures of the hand preserved without distortion, missing digits, or extra fingers?
Is the faint soft tissue shadow outlining the hand preserved in the background, rather than being erased or obscured?
Is the ring scaled proportionally to the finger bone, appearing physically fitted rather than oversized or microscopic?
Does the ring band appear to wrap around the bone (showing depth/encirclement) rather than floating flat on top of the image?
Is the ring oriented with the gemstone facing the viewer, consistent with the hand's dorsal view?
Is the background free from intrusive elements from the ring's source image, such as velvet box textures or studio lighting gradients?
Is the junction between the photorealistic gemstone and the X-ray metal band seamless, forming a single coherent object without jarring breaks?
Is the image free from obvious compositing artifacts like rectangular halos, blurriness, or visible seams around the ring?
Is the image free from hallucinations such as duplicate rings, floating jewelry parts, or disjointed bones?
